channel.title: Welcome on Josie Binkley
channel.description: Recent content in Welcome on Josie Binkley

item.title: Building This Hugo Site While Feeling Lost
item.description:
I started this project feeling completely overwhelmed. Hugo looked fast and clean, but the folder structure and templates were confusing at first.

What helped most was breaking it into tiny steps:

  1. Make one page render correctly.
  2. Reuse a shared layout.
  3. Add navigation links.
  4. Improve styling after the structure works.

That approach turned a vague task into a series of clear wins. If you’re stuck, do the smallest possible next step and keep moving.

item.title: Projects
item.description:
A selection of research, operations, and communications projects representing my full experience.

Multi-Phase Neuromodulation Project

  • Role: Research Assistant, Del Negro Lab (William & Mary)
  • Dates: 2023 to 2024
  • Scope: Co-led planning and execution for a multi-phase neuromodulation study, including timeline management, meeting coordination, and project documentation.
  • My Involvement: Performed neurosurgical implantation and microinjection procedures in mice, completed histology-related workflow support and documentation, coordinated cross-team workflows, and maintained records for analysis/manuscript preparation.
  • Impact: Improved project continuity, strengthened surgical and post-procedure documentation quality, and supported on-time delivery of key research milestones.

Electrophysiology and Mouse CNS Methods Project

  • Role: Research Assistant, Del Negro Lab (William & Mary)
  • Dates: 2022 to 2023
  • Scope: Conducted electrophysiological recordings and neonatal mouse CNS dissections using vibratome sectioning and electrophysiology rigs.
  • My Involvement: Executed hands-on lab methods, maintained technical records for reproducibility, and communicated findings to multidisciplinary collaborators.
  • Impact: Expanded the lab’s technical capacity for electrophysiology workflows and improved reproducibility through clearer protocols.

Research Operations and Compliance Support

  • Role: Research Assistant, Schniepp Lab (William & Mary)
  • Dates: August 2025 to Present
  • Scope: Supported research operations through reproducibility-focused records, protocol compliance, and coordination of deliverables with faculty and lab teams.
  • My Involvement: Designed and 3D-printed diatom models, applied microscopy and coding in workflow support, and presented research to technical and non-technical audiences.
  • Impact: Improved documentation quality and communication across stakeholders while helping standardize project handoffs.

Budget and Resource Management Initiative

  • Role: Treasurer, Phi Sigma Pi (William & Mary)
  • Dates: November 2023 to December 2024
  • Scope: Managed chapter budget operations, expense tracking, and financial reporting for events and programming.
  • My Involvement: Oversaw resource allocation decisions and built reporting that supported leadership planning.
  • Impact: Increased fiscal visibility and supported more accountable, data-informed spending.

Social Media Hosting and Student Engagement (@lifeatwm)

  • Role: Social Media Host / Content Contributor, Life at WM (Instagram)
  • Dates: February 17, 2025 to February 22, 2025
  • Scope: Created and hosted student-focused Instagram content to highlight daily life, events, and community at William & Mary.
  • My Involvement: Planned content themes, hosted story/post segments, and adapted messaging for audience engagement.
  • Impact: Strengthened student-facing communication and highlighted campus culture for prospective and current students.

item.title: Resume
item.description:
Josephine Binkley
Williamsburg, VA
josiebinkley@outlook.com | 571-919-0355

Summary

Neuroscience student at William & Mary (May 2026) with combined experience in research execution, lab operations, project coordination, and workflow support. Hands-on background includes electrophysiology, neuromodulation, documentation/compliance, and cross-functional team collaboration in fast-paced academic and community settings.

Education

William & Mary
B.S. Neuroscience, May 2026
Major GPA: 3.71 | Cumulative GPA: 3.48

Skills

Laboratory and Technical

  • Electrophysiology, stereotactic surgery, microinjection, neuromodulation
  • PCR/genotyping, fluorescent and electron microscopy, vibratome sectioning
  • Vivarium and animal colony management

Operations and Project Support
